tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 1]**

(a) A cruise ship travels 2067 km.

(i) (b) Write 2067 in words.

(c) Write 2067 correct to the nearest hundred.

(d) When full, the cruise ship carries 880 guests and 360 crew.

Write the ratio guests : crew in its simplest form.

 : 

(e) There are 480 cabins on the ship.
On one cruise, 456 of these cabins were used.

Find the percentage of cabins that were used.

 \%

(f) Last year the cost of a cruise was \$4600.
This year the cost of the same cruise is \$4784.

Work out the percentage increase in the cost.

 \%

(g) The cost of building the ship was \$153 000 000.

Write 153 000 000 in standard form.

(h) There are 480 cabins on the ship.
There are four types of cabin: Ocean-view, Balcony, Interior and Suite.
Hannah starts to draw a pie chart to show the numbers of each type of cabin.

(i) (i) Show that there are 120 Ocean-view cabins on the ship.

(j) The table shows information about each type of cabin.

\begin{tabular}{l l l}
Type of cabin & Number of cabins & Sector angle in a pie chart \\
Ocean-view & 120 & 90° \\
Balcony & 192 & 144° \\
Interior & 68 & \\
Suite & 100 & \\
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.

(b) Complete the pie chart.
texHtml:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 1]** (a) A cruise ship travels 2067 km. (i) (b) Write 2067 in words. (c) Write 2067 correct to the nearest hundred. (d) When full, the cruise ship carries 880 guests and 360 crew. Write the ratio guests : crew in its simplest form. : (e) There are 480 cabins on the ship. On one cruise, 456 of these cabins were used. Find the percentage of cabins that were used. \% (f) Last year the cost of a cruise was \$4600. This year the cost of the same cruise is \$4784. Work out the percentage increase in the cost. \% (g) The cost of building the ship was \$153 000 000. Write 153 000 000 in standard form. (h) There are 480 cabins on the ship. There are four types of cabin: Ocean-view, Balcony, Interior and Suite. Hannah starts to draw a pie chart to show the numbers of each type of cabin. (i) (i) Show that there are 120 Ocean-view cabins on the ship. (j) The table shows information about each type of cabin.
Type of cabinNumber of cabinsSector angle in a pie chart
Ocean-view12090°
Balcony192144°
Interior68
Suite100
(a) Complete the table. (b) Complete the pie chart.

tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 2]**

(a) Using numbers from 55 to 85, write down

(i) (b) a multiple of 23,

(c) a factor of 120,

(d) a common multiple of 8 and 12,

(e) a number that is both square and odd,

(f) a number that has exactly 2 factors.

(g) Write 220 as the product of its prime factors.

**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 3]**

(a) Complete the table of values for $y = 1 + 5x - x^2$.

\begin{tabular}{c c c c c c c}
$x$ & -1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
$y$ &  & 1 & 5 &  & 7 &  \\
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 1 + 5x - x^2$ for $-1 \leq x \leq 5$.

(c) On the grid, draw the line $y = 3$.

Use your line to solve the equation $1 + 5x - x^2 = 3$.

$x =$ ...\ or $x =$ ...

tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 4]**

(a) The diagram shows the plan of part of Rachel's garden.

Calculate the area.

(b) Rachel has a pond in her garden in the shape of a circle.
The circumference of the pond is 4.25 m.

Calculate the diameter of the pond.
Give your answer in centimetres.

(c) A plant pot is a cylinder with radius 15 cm and height 24 cm.

Calculate the volume of the pot.

(d) The diagram shows two mathematically similar plant pots.

The smaller pot has height 21.6 cm and diameter 27 cm.
The larger pot has diameter 33 cm.

Find the height, $h$, of the larger pot.

(e) A shop sells bags of compost in three different sizes.

\begin{tabular}{l l l}
Small & 30 litres & \$5.82 \\
Medium & 50 litres & \$9.45 \\
Large & 75 litres & \$14.50 \\
\end{tabular}

Work out which size of bag gives the best value.
Show how you decide.
texHtml:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 4]** (a) The diagram shows the plan of part of Rachel's garden. Calculate the area. (b) Rachel has a pond in her garden in the shape of a circle. The circumference of the pond is 4.25 m. Calculate the diameter of the pond. Give your answer in centimetres. (c) A plant pot is a cylinder with radius 15 cm and height 24 cm. Calculate the volume of the pot. (d) The diagram shows two mathematically similar plant pots. The smaller pot has height 21.6 cm and diameter 27 cm. The larger pot has diameter 33 cm. Find the height, $h$, of the larger pot. (e) A shop sells bags of compost in three different sizes.
Small30 litres\$5.82
Medium50 litres\$9.45
Large75 litres\$14.50
Work out which size of bag gives the best value. Show how you decide.

tex:
The table shows the maximum power, kW, and the time taken, in seconds, to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h for each of 10 cars.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Maximum power (kW) & 77 & 52 & 103 & 55 & 44 & 51 & 85 & 135 & 90 & 110 \\
\hline
Time (seconds) & 12.5 & 14.9 & 9.0 & 12.1 & 14.4 & 12.9 & 10.0 & 7.1 & 11.0 & 9.4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i)  Find the range of the times.

 Find the median maximum power.

(b) (i)  Complete the scatter diagram.\\
The first eight points have been plotted for you.

 What type of correlation is shown on the scatter diagram?

 Describe the relationship between the maximum power of a car and the time taken to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h.

 Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.

 Another car has a maximum power of 63\,kW.\\
Use your line of best fit to estimate the time taken for this car to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h.

(c) Robert buys a car for \$18\,160.\\
He pays a deposit of \$6460.\\
He pays the rest of the money in 24 equal monthly payments.

Work out the amount of each monthly payment.

(d) A fuel tank holds 52 litres when full.\\
The tank is a quarter full.\\
Jim fills the tank with fuel that costs \$2.18 per litre.

Work out how much Jim pays.
texHtml:
The table shows the maximum power, kW, and the time taken, in seconds, to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h for each of 10 cars.
Maximum power (kW)77521035544518513590110
Time (seconds)12.514.99.012.114.412.910.07.111.09.4
(a) (i) Find the range of the times. Find the median maximum power. (b) (i) Complete the scatter diagram.\\ The first eight points have been plotted for you. What type of correlation is shown on the scatter diagram? Describe the relationship between the maximum power of a car and the time taken to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h. Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram. Another car has a maximum power of 63\,kW.\\ Use your line of best fit to estimate the time taken for this car to accelerate from 0 to 100\,km/h. (c) Robert buys a car for \$18\,160.\\ He pays a deposit of \$6460.\\ He pays the rest of the money in 24 equal monthly payments. Work out the amount of each monthly payment. (d) A fuel tank holds 52 litres when full.\\ The tank is a quarter full.\\ Jim fills the tank with fuel that costs \$2.18 per litre. Work out how much Jim pays.

tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 6]**

(a) On the grid, draw the image of triangle A after:
(i) a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}$, [2]
(ii) a reflection in the line $x = 3$. [2]

(iii) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B. [3]

(b) $\mathbf{a} = \begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$, $\mathbf{b} = \begin{pmatrix} 5 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix}$, $\mathbf{c} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 4 \end{pmatrix}$.

(i) $\mathbf{a} + \mathbf{b}$ [1]
(ii) $\mathbf{b} - 2\mathbf{c}$ [2]

(c) Point P has coordinates (6, -2) and PQ = $\begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
Find the coordinates of point Q.

(, )

(a) $W = 3a + 5c$

Find the value of $W$ when $a = 6$ and $c = 2$.

(b) Factorise completely.
\[12b + 8b^2\]

(c) Make $m$ the subject of the formula $y = 4m - p$.

(d) Find the value of $x$ when $5^x \times 5^3 = 5^{12}$.

(e) Find the value of

(i)  $3^0$,

 $5^{-2}$.

(f) In this part, all measurements are in centimetres.

The diagram shows a kite with sides $(2x + 5)$ and $(3x - 1)$.\\
The perimeter of the kite is 33\,cm.

Work out the length of a shorter side.

(a) 
$P$ and $Q$ are points on the circle, centre $O$.\\
$APB$ is a tangent to the circle at $P$.

(i)  Write down the mathematical name for the line $PQ$.

 Explain why angle $OPB$ is $90°$.

 Find the value of $x$.

(b) 
The diagram shows two parallel lines and two straight lines.

(i)  Find the value of $a$.\\
Give a reason for your answer.

$a =$ ...\ because ...

 Find the value of $b$.\\
Give a reason for your answer.

$b =$ ...\ because ...

 Find the value of $c$.

(c) 
Calculate the value of $x$.

A sequence of patterns is made using black counters and white counters.

(a) Draw Pattern 4.

(b) Complete the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Pattern & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\
\hline
Number of black counters & 4 & 6 & 8 & & \\
\hline
Number of white counters & 1 & 4 & 9 & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(c) Write an expression, in terms of $n$, for

(i)  the number of black counters in Pattern $n$,

 the number of white counters in Pattern $n$.

(d) Elena has 30 black counters and 140 white counters.

Can she make Pattern 12 using her counters?\\
Explain your answer.

...\ because ...
